perturbation.original_text: Nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring any rights by license, express, implied or otherwise, for any information, discovery or improvements made, conceived, or acquired after the date of this Agreement, or for any invention, discovery, or improvement made, conceived, or acquired after the date of this Agreement, or for any invention, discovery, or improvement made, conceived or acquired prior to the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring any rights by license, express, implied or otherwise, for any information, discovery or improvements made, conceived, or acquired after the date of this Agreement, except for those improvements jointly developed by both parties. For any invention, discovery, or improvement made, conceived or acquired prior to the date of this Agreement, a non-exclusive license is granted to the Receiving Party for internal use.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by carving out exceptions to the 'No License Granted' clause. It introduces that improvements jointly developed are now subject to licensing (the terms are undefined), and pre-existing inventions grant a non-exclusive license, conflicting with the original blanket prohibition. This creates ambiguity and uncertainty about the actual licensing terms within the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No License Granted . Nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring any rights by license, express, implied or otherwise, for any information, discovery or improvements made, conceived, or acquired after the date of this Agreement, except for those improvements jointly developed by both parties. For any invention, discovery, or improvement made, conceived or acquired prior to the date of this Agreement, a non-exclusive license is granted to the Receiving Party for internal use.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that no rights are granted by license for any information, discovery or improvements. The changed text introduces exceptions to this rule, creating a non-exclusive license for pre-existing inventions and licensing terms for jointly developed improvements. This directly contradicts the original blanket prohibition, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The contradicted text and the Changed Text are located in the same section, but that doesn't change the fact that a contradiction was introduced.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information shall be used solely for the purpose of sharing technical, business and financial information to foster potential mutually beneficial business relationships.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information shall be used solely for the purpose of sharing technical, business and financial information to foster potential mutually beneficial business relationships, unless otherwise permitted by the Disclosing Party in writing, including for marketing purposes.
perturbation.explanation: The original text restricts the use of confidential information to fostering business relationships. The modified text adds an exception: 'unless otherwise permitted by the Disclosing Party in writing, including for marketing purposes.' This directly contradicts the 'solely' restriction, allowing for broader use (e.g., marketing) with written permission. This introduces ambiguity as the definition of mutually beneficial business relationships is subjective.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Limitation on Use and Disclosure of Confidential Information . Confidential Information shall be used solely for the purpose of sharing technical, business and financial information to foster potential mutually beneficial business relationships. (a) Confidential Information shall not be copied or reproduced by the Receiving Party, except for such copies as may be reasonably required for accomplishment of the purposes stated herein; (b) Confidential Information shall be disclosed only to employees, agents, and other parties of the Receiving Party who have a “need to know” in connection with the purposes stated herein; and (c) This Agreement shall not restrict the disclosure or use of information that: (i) was in the public domain at the time of disclosure or thereafter enters the public domain through no breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party; (ii) was, at the time of the receipt by the Receiving Party, otherwise known to the Receiving Party without restrictions as to the use or disclosure; (iii) becomes known to the Receiving Party from a source other than the disclosing party without breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party; or (iv) is developed independently by the Receiving Party and without reliance upon the Confidential Information disclosed herein.
perturbation.justification: The original text and the contradicted text both state that Confidential Information 'shall be used solely for the purpose of sharing technical, business and financial information to foster potential mutually beneficial business relationships.' The changed text adds 'unless otherwise permitted by the Disclosing Party in writing, including for marketing purposes.' This creates a direct contradiction because it allows for uses beyond the 'solely' stated purpose, which is a strong and direct conflict with the contradicted text and the original text.

perturbation.original_text: The decision of the arbitrator shall be final, conclusive and binding on the parties to the arbitration.
perturbation.changed_text: The decision of the arbitrator shall be final, conclusive and binding on the parties to the arbitration, except in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct by the arbitrator.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that the arbitrator's decision is final and binding. The modification introduces an exception: 'except in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct by the arbitrator.' This directly contradicts the 'final and binding' nature of the decision, as it implies that the decision can be challenged or overturned under those specific circumstances. It introduces uncertainty as determining 'gross negligence or willful misconduct' is subjective and can lead to further legal disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Arbitration and Equitable Relief. (a) Arbitration . Except as provided herein, the Parties agree that any dispute or controversy arising out of or relating to any interpretation, construction, performance or breach of this Agreement shall be settled by arbitration to be held in San Diego County, California, USA in accordance with the Commercial Arbitration Rules then in effect for the American Arbitration Association. The arbitrator may grant injunctions or other relief in such dispute or controversy. In the event of arbitration, the Parties may undertake a reasonable amount of discovery. The decision of the arbitrator shall be final, conclusive and binding on the parties to the arbitration, except in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct by the arbitrator. Judgement may be entered on the arbitrator’s decision in any court having jurisdiction. (b) Equitable Remedies . The Parties agree that it would be impossible or inadequate to measure and calculate damages from any breach of the covenants set forth herein. Accordingly, the Parties agree that if in the event of a breach of the covenants contained in this Agreement, the affected party will have available, in addition to any other right or remedy available, the right to obtain an injunction from a court of competent jurisdiction restraining such breach or threatened breach and to specify performance of any such provision of the Agreement. The Parties further agree that no bond or other security shall be required in obtaining such equitable relief and the Parties hereby consent to the issuance of such injunction and to the ordering of specific performance.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original clause, which states that the arbitrator's decision is final and binding. The addition of 'except in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct by the arbitrator' introduces an exception that directly contradicts the 'final and binding' nature of the decision. The contradicted text section, 'Arbitration and Equitable Relief', also contains the same sentence 'The decision of the arbitrator shall be final, conclusive and binding on the parties to the arbitration, except in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct by the arbitrator.' which has the same issue. The modification introduces uncertainty as determining 'gross negligence or willful misconduct' is subjective and can lead to further legal disputes, therefore this is a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement contains the entire understanding between the Parties concerning the subject matter hereof, superseding all prior contemporaneous communications, agreements and understandings between the Parties with respect to the disclosure and protection of Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement contains the entire understanding between the Parties concerning the subject matter hereof, superseding all prior contemporaneous communications, agreements and understandings between the Parties with respect to the disclosure and protection of Confidential Information, except for any separate agreements regarding intellectual property rights.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that the agreement represents the 'entire understanding', superseding all prior agreements. The added exception 'except for any separate agreements regarding intellectual property rights' contradicts this. It implies that there may be other, unwritten or separate, legally binding agreements concerning IP, directly conflicting with the 'entire understanding' clause and adding uncertainty to the scope of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Entire Understanding . This Agreement contains the entire understanding between the Parties concerning the subject matter hereof, superseding all prior contemporaneous communications, agreements and understandings between the Parties with respect to the disclosure and protection of Confidential Information, except for any separate agreements regarding intellectual property rights. The rights and obligations of the Parties shall be limited to those expressly set forth herein. nStor Corporation, Inc. Xyratex Technology Limited /s/ Steve Aleshire /s/ Matt Cornell Authorized Signature Authorized Signature Matt Cornell Steve Aleshire Print Name Print Name Executive VP – Storage Systems COO Title Title September 3, 2004 9/3/04 Date Date 3
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original clause, which stated the agreement contains the entire understanding, by adding 'except for any separate agreements regarding intellectual property rights.' The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, highlighting the introduction of a contradiction within the agreement itself. The addition creates a clear in-text contradiction because it implies the existence of other legally binding agreements concerning IP, which directly conflicts with the 'entire understanding' clause. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type, as it introduces conflicting terms regarding the scope of the agreement, creating uncertainty about which terms should be enforced.
